meta_notes: Feeds fresh (extract 2026-07-15 09:15Z). News shelf intentionally empty: top candidates failed date-verification — bemotrizinol FDA approval broke 2026-06-09 (>7d, the documented stale lesson); the 'FDA sunscreen recall' story is dated 2025-07-11 per its Newsweek dateline (FDA enforcement report 2025-07-10) — a year old; the viral 'sunscreen quadruples melanoma' claim is saturated in-feed (her live myth-buster + a tracked creator) and folded into the sun-protection theme. No verifiable <7d derm news; not padded.

her_coverage.summary: Deep skin-cancer library (BCC ultimate guide 493K, '3 facts about skin cancer' 185K, Feb-'26 warning signs) but nothing recent and none in this rapid-quiz format.

themes.corpus_position: Skin cancer is extremely common — melanoma plus non-melanoma types like basal cell and squamous cell; dermatologists use the 'ugly duckling sign' to spot the mole that looks different from all the rest.

angles.angle: Run the 5s quiz, then land the ABCDE + ugly-duckling rule
Melanoma vs seborrheic keratosis vs skin tag — spot the difference
Myth-bust 'non-melanoma is no big deal' with your large-SCC case

videos.caption: 1. Sunscreen doesn’t cause cancer. The myth that sunscreen causes cancer evolves from evidence that some chemical sunscreens get absorbed th

videos.author: beautydermmd
videos.caption: Not her serum. Not her skincare. HER HABITS. 👀 Sun protection is elite behavior. UPF clothing. Wide-brim hat. Sunglasses. Shade. This is wha

videos.author: drspf
videos.caption: The sun shirt kids were right all along. 🤙 Sun protection is smart, evidence-based, and anti-aging- let’s not go backwards in our thinking s

her_coverage.summary: Strong and current — your 'sunscreen doesn't cause cancer' video is live (174K, 3d), on top of the sunscreen Q&A and '3 facts about skin cancer' (185K).

themes.corpus_position: The myth that sunscreen causes cancer comes from misreading absorption studies — sunscreen protects against the UV damage that actually causes skin cancer. Dermatologists take sun protection very seriously because we see the damage it does.

angles.angle: Myth-bust 'sunscreen causes cancer' with the absorption-study facts
Habits over serums: UPF clothing, shade, reapplication
Debunk the viral 'sunscreen quadruples melanoma risk' claim — confounded correlation

themes.name: Vaseline & barrier-cream hacks (occlusives)
themes.receipt: dermarkologist '4 Vaseline hacks' (743K, 42×, 1% ♥) + dermguru barrier-cream magnet (3.0M, ♥ gold) — both 73s explainers

videos.author: dermarkologist
videos.caption: 4 Vaseline hacks that I swear by as your favorite dermatologist! What is your favorite use of Vaseline? Let me know ⬇️ Original vid: @Alexa

videos.author: dermguru
videos.caption: Comment BARRIER CREAM and I’ll send you a few skincare options to heal your barrier! If diaper paste is intimidating and you’re not ready to

her_coverage.summary: Rich Vaseline/occlusive catalog — chapped-lips (2M), 'slugging with this much you're doing it wrong' duet (157K), 'don't layer over actives' — but newest is Dec-'25 (milia).

themes.corpus_position: Petroleum jelly (Aquaphor/Vaseline) is an occlusive — apply it right out of the shower to trap moisture. For slugging you only need a pea-sized amount, and never put it over active ingredients like retinoids, AHAs or BHAs.

angles.angle: React to '4 Vaseline hacks': co-sign the good, flag layering over actives
Slugging done right: pea-sized only, skip it over retinoids
Barrier-cream 101: when Vaseline heals vs when it just traps

stitch_shortlist.author: @tierra5239
stitch_shortlist.claim: UV cooling sleeves are summer skin protection

pitch.the_claim: Cooling arm sleeves protect your skin from the sun all summer.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 3.8M views at peak summer and it's photoprotection — your lane. The claim is half-right: UPF sleeves work but miss face/ears/hands and give false security (borderline: it's a product/lifestyle post, not a derm).
pitch.your_fit: You take sun protection 'very seriously' and preach UPF clothing plus reapplication — a clean yes-and.
angles: yes-and: sleeves are smart — here's the UPF number to look for
the spots sleeves miss: face, ears, back of hands
false security: clothing isn't a pass to skip SPF

pitch.the_claim: A proudly chaotic multi-step skincare routine (2.3M views).
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2.3M views and 2K comments — people are already debating it; a chaotic routine is prime derm-react bait.
pitch.your_fit: You teach 'less is more' and layering order (clean-routine + anti-aging series) — react with what to cut and why.
angles: empathy-first: love the enthusiasm — here's what to drop
layering order that won't wreck your barrier
the 3 steps that actually matter

pitch.the_claim: 'SPF stan til I die' — an enthusiastic chemical-SPF-50 superfan.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 906K and climbing at peak summer; a rare pro-sunscreen viral you can amplify as the antidote to the 'sunscreen causes cancer' wave. Miss: it's already correct, so this reinforces rather than debunks.
pitch.your_fit: Your live 'sunscreen doesn't cause cancer' video and sunscreen Q&A make you the perfect co-sign.
angles: yes-and: stan louder — here's why chemical filters are safe
chemical vs mineral: both work, pick what you'll reapply
pair with the myth-bust — this is the antidote to the fear-mongering

pitch.why_it_stitches: 884K and climbing; PDRN ('salmon DNA') is the hype ingredient of 2026 — first-in-lane opportunity.
pitch.your_fit: NEEDS YOUR TAKE — no PDRN in your corpus (white space). You can be the derm who explains what PDRN actually is and whether a topical does anything.

collagen creams: the molecule's too big to absorb

pitch.the_claim: 'Ponds > retinol uglies' — use cold cream instead of retinoids.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 854K; a direct, debatable claim pitting a drugstore moisturizer against the anti-aging ingredient with the most evidence.
pitch.your_fit: Deep retinoid corpus — 'Tretinoin vs retinol' guide and the 'which retinoid to use' series; you can explain the 'retinol uglies' (purge/irritation) and how to avoid them.
angles: empathy: the 'retinol uglies' are real — here's how to skip them
Ponds moisturizes; retinol remodels — different jobs
the slow-start routine that prevents the uglies
